spectral_blur.description: Very short source (click), narrow mid band, Halo 3000ms, Residual 0 → continuous ghost bed at -30dB.
Play a chord → hit Freeze → automate frequency-range low/high up the spectrum → harmonic cloud morphs in pitch-space.
Apply to a short stab with long Halo → get reverse-reverb-like build-in without needing to flip the audio.

pitch_hack.description: Short delay (~50ms), Feedback 0.4, Randomize 0.6 → every hi-hat hit gets 2-3 unpredictable pitched ghost echoes.
Chord stab → Reverse on, Transpose +3st, Feedback 0.5 → pitched reverse ghost trails fade out after each stab.
Put on a clave or click → Randomize full → each hit decays into a different pitched echo pattern.

pitchloop89.description: Put on a chord stab → Pitch A=0, Fine A=+5, Pitch B=0, Fine B=-7, Feedback both at 0.4 → instant detuned shimmer cloud that hangs after the stab.
Enable Reverse on both voices, short delay time → chord swells backwards in before the downbeat. Haunting Monolake effect.
Feed a single click → Feedback ~0.92, Pitch A=+12, Pitch B=0 → infinite ascending cascade. Classic Henke.
Slow Position LFO with random waveform → delay buffer warps around, producing organic pitched ghosts that never repeat.

snipper.description: Route vocal → Snipper → short snippet, fast replay, pitch mod → instant Akufen-adjacent micro-vocal pattern.
Closed hat → Snipper with tiny snippet length, pitch mod ±3 semitones, randomize on → every hat is pitched differently. Microhouse signature.

re_enveloper.description: Low + mid bands at -3.0 C/E (expansion toward short decay), short release → 4-bar pad becomes punchy 1/4-note stab.
High band 2.5 expansion, slow release → snare crack pops out without affecting kick or toms.

expressive_chords.description: Pick post-rock set → let only top 2-3 voices through (gate the others) → sparse open voicings that work as minimal chord stabs.

expressive_chords.technique: MPE micro-tuning drift on held chord
expressive_chords.description: Hold a chord → MPE pitch-bend each voice independently → organic detuning drift.

retrigger.description: Select 4 hi-hat notes at end of section → Retrigger count 16, exponential curve → accelerating roll into the downbeat.
Single snare hit → Retrigger 8 times with linear-to-long decay → natural-feeling fill.

filler.description: Dial density, click generate, commit the pattern you like. Move on — don't spend time.
Density at 20-30%, kick-snare-hat only, randomize → random sparse pattern → pick one as the minimal starting point for further human editing.

patterns_iftah.description: Disable snare + open hat → Generate until pattern feels right → all voices at low density → immediate Villalobos-adjacent groove.
Use for fast first-draft drums across any genre — commit the one that fits, adjust by hand.

phase_pattern.description: Low Timing Offset (0.15), Quantize off → every hit drifts slightly from grid in a coherent, groove-affirming way (no random chaos).
Apply to a vocal chop clip → phase-coherent timing/pitch shifts → chops feel recorded not programmed.

stages.description: Single C1 note → Stages with 8-stage pattern, varied repeat counts, modest pitch offsets → coherent 16-bar bassline with motion.

stages.technique: Melodic fill from a single hook note
stages.description: Apply to melodic element at section-end → Stages generates a 2-bar fill with repeats + rests that feels composed.

microtuner.description: Load a 19-tone equal-temperament (19-TET) Scala file → play a simple motif → immediately off-grid harmonic character without sounding random.
Load Harry Partch's 43-tone just-intonation scale → apply to Drone Lab's Harmonic Drone Generator → ancient-sounding sustained bed.
Deck A = major scale, Deck B = 19-TET. Automate Blend from 0 to 1 over 16 bars → harmony literally reshapes in time.
Load a 24-TET scale + ROLI-style MPE controller → slides and bends fall onto microtonal grid for non-Western expressivity.

slice_shuffler.description: Clean 8-bar kick pattern → Slice Shuffler with 8 slices → randomize slice 4 and slice 7 only → ghost-note drift every ~1 minute of playback.
16-bar loop → 4 slices → reverse slice 3 only → single bar of reverse drums as a momentary disruption.

sq_sequencer.description: Melody lane 16 steps, rhythm lane 15 steps, bass lane 11 steps → sequences drift in and out of phase over hundreds of bars, never repeating exactly.
Snare lane with condition 'every 3rd pass' → snare appears only once per ~16 bars → organic ghost pattern.

polyrhythm.description: Voice 1 = 16 steps / 4 pulses (kick), Voice 2 = 11 steps / 3 pulses (ghost snare), Voice 3 = 13 steps / 7 pulses (hat). Generates pattern that never repeats exactly for hundreds of bars.
All 6 voices at sparse density (1-3 pulses each) with asymmetric lengths → organic feel that evolves over minutes.

bell_tower.description: Short decay, subtle modulator depth, single hit per bar, through a long cathedral IR → the thin bell stab that recurs in Arpiar tracks.
Modulator ratio around 1.41 (irrational), short decay, through dual-detune PitchLoop89 (+5 / -7 cents) → phased bell cluster.

bass.description: Sine+triangle, fundamental C1, amp envelope long decay, subtle pitch envelope down 1 semitone over 100ms. Cleaner than sampled 808s, and tunable in-key.
Two detuned oscillators octave apart, LP filter resonant, saturation on output. The sum-of-beats IS the movement — no LFO needed.
Pure sine at fundamental, very long release, no transient. The bass is felt not heard — pair with a short percussive layer (click at 2-5kHz) for attack.
Sidechain compression is not just for the kick-bass pump. It's a modulation source.
Don't think about tracks — think about frequency bands. Each band is a voice in the mix.

wavetable.description: Saw wavetable, voices=7, spread=0.35, subosc square octave up, LPF fully open, chorus via unison detune not effect. Aim for glued breadth not comb-filter thinness.
Two wavetables (e.g. Formant + Noise), wavetable-position modulated by slow LFO (0.05 Hz), filter envelope negative so tone opens during decay. No notes change — the TIMBRE is the music.
Square-ish wavetable, LFO to filter cutoff synced at 1/8 or 1/16, depth near max. Classic dubstep bass — position automation over 4 bars moves texture under the wobble.

analog.description: Two detuned saws at different octaves, LP24 filter with slight resonance, no LFO — let the beat frequency between the oscillators do the modulation.
Single saw, slow filter sweep from dark to bright across 32 bars, filter env amount slight negative so attack is dark — the pad evolves by unfolding, not by adding.
Osc1 saw, octave down, LP24 with high resonance (0.8+), env amount 0.9, amp decay short. Use Filter1 Freq + Res as performance controls — the acid line IS the filter gesture.
The difference between "sounds like a computer" and "sounds alive" is modulation that operates below conscious perception.

meld.description: Two Drift engines with different characters (one dark, one bright), crossfade-modulated by slow LFO. The pad morphs between timbres without notes changing.
Engine A: mid bass with character. Engine B: pure sine sub. Balanced -3 dB apart. Two engines = precise crossover without needing a split.
Engine A: plucked pluck at root. Engine B: sustained pad at 3rd/5th. Stacked voicing without needing two tracks.

sting_iftah.description: Detune heavily, drop pitch low, Density ~15%, low-pass filter → 'acid line' becomes a warm mutating sub-bass that's never the same two bars in a row.
Default patch, full Type Morph to acid, mid density → classic acid line; use 3D morph mapped to a performance controller.

collision.description: Struck resonator, material=wood, partials=5-8, decay short. Marimba/vibraphone without sampling — the physical model responds to velocity like the real instrument.
Struck bar, inharmonicity high, decay long, LFO modulating partial levels. The bell never settles — each strike reveals a different harmonic slice.
Bowed string exciter with mute on, low tension, short decay — prepared-piano territory. Pair with Convolution Reverb for Cage/Feldman atmosphere.

drift.description: Sine+triangle mix, short-attack amp, dark filter, gentle chorus send. Not trying to emulate Rhodes — trying to SIT where Rhodes sits in the mix.
Sine sub at C1, triangle octave up blended -6 dB, LP filter fixed open, no modulation. The bass doesn't move — the filter on the REVERB send does.
Saw+triangle, detune 0.1, slow filter envelope, long amp release, subtle vibrato at 5 Hz. 80s pad territory without the period-drama cliché.
The difference between "sounds like a computer" and "sounds alive" is modulation that operates below conscious perception.
Real producers don't draw straight lines or perfect curves. Their automation reflects human movement.

sampler.description: 4 velocity layers per note (pp/mp/mf/ff), velocity->filter positive so harder hits open brighter. Essential for sampled orchestral realism — anything else sounds like a toy keyboard.
2-3 sample layers per key, detune each slightly, filter each differently. One sample = thin; three samples stacked = lush ensemble without needing a real choir.
Multi-sample kick/snare/hat with 3-4 round-robin variations per note. Humanizes programmed drums — no two hits sound identical without needing micro-timing.

poli.description: 2 saws detuned 7 cents, sub-osc octave down, low-pass with slight resonance, chorus send heavy. 80s Juno territory without the preset.
Single saw, fast filter envelope, high resonance, short decay. Classic 1987 house stab — works on top of any groove.
Saw+square, slow attack, chorus, subtle LFO on cutoff. Where Poli shines vs Analog: less clinical, more 'plastic-fantastic' 80s charm.

vector_fm.description: Carrier at C4, modulator at inharmonic ratio, Vector X controlling modulator depth, Vector Y controlling feedback. Particle motion makes the bell evolve physically.
Low carrier (C1), high modulator, Vector coupled to both. Sounds like a large resonant object being struck and allowed to decay over 30 seconds.
Carrier at fundamental, modulator LFO'd by Vector X, amp LFO'd by Vector Y. FM bass with physical 'breathing' rather than square-wave LFO control.

impulse.description: Drag 8 samples onto the pads → play. No chain complexity. Great for sketching before moving to Drum Rack.
Put closed and open hats in the same mute group → playing the open hat kills the closed hat tail. Natural choke behavior.
In experimental electronic music, effects chains ARE instruments.

operator.description: Algorithm 5 (two parallel 2-op stacks), operator ratios 1:3.51 and 1:14, envelope decay long. The inharmonic ratio 3.51 is the classic bell metallic quality.
Single sine carrier, modulator at ratio 1:1 low feedback, fundamental at C1. The sine fundamental is what reproduces on laptop speakers via harmonics — FM adds body without touching fundamental.
Algorithm with 3 serial carriers, slow LFO modulating operator levels, long release. Shifts timbre over 16+ bars without notes changing. The 'breathing' is the composition.

simpler.description: Load vocal, slicing mode, slice per transient, assign pads to short syllable fragments. Program MIDI pattern on the slices — the rhythmic chopping IS the vocal line.
One-shot mode, pitched sub sample in C1, warp off, amp envelope short. Layer this UNDER your kick — the sub_low (20-60 Hz) band carries the weight.

Slicing mode on a classic break (e.g. Amen, Funky Drummer), slice per transient, then program MIDI with slight timing offsets (+/-10 ticks) — Dilla's micro-drunk feel comes from MIDI, not groove templates.

electric.description: Suitcase preset, tremolo at 5 Hz, chorus modest. The tine-bar mechanism is already in the model — no outboard reverb needed for the classic sound.
Wurly preset, short attack, amp drive in the 20-30% range, high-pass at 100 Hz. The reed-bar grit IS the character — don't EQ it out.
Rhodes through Grain Delay + Auto Filter LFO, slight pitch detune. Boards of Canada / lo-fi hip-hop territory — the imperfection is the sound.

granulator_iii.description: Load any vocal snippet or field recording → Cloud mode, Grain Size 200-500ms, Position Random 0.3, Pitch Random 0.1 → immediate organic evolving bed.
Load a drum break → Loop mode, sync to clip tempo → automate Position in short jumps → Akufen-style micro-edit patterns without manual slicing.
Sustained vowel sample → Cloud mode, Pitch Random 7st, Grain Size 400ms → vocal becomes harmonic cloud without recognizable words.
Set up Granulator on a return track, route any source to it, hit Capture, then grain the just-captured audio. Zero commitment to the source — if you don't like the grain, record a new slice.

emit.description: Load a sustained pad source, grain density high, pitch randomization slight, reverb send heavy. The grains blur the source into an undifferentiated textural bed.
Source = a droning note, grain density medium, randomize position slightly. The bass evolves organically without automation.
Short vocal sample as source, grain density sparse, position LFO slow, pitch spread wide. Not a chord, not a noise — a liminal texture that sits between.

harmonic_drone_generator.description: Just intonation tuning, all 8 voices active but voiced as a sparse cluster (root + 5 + minor 3 + octave), level at -30 dB → sits under the whole track as a harmonic shadow.
Indonesian Pelog scale, 5 voices, long reverb send → off-grid harmonic character that sounds ancient without sounding 'wrong'.
Automate Detune over 32 bars from 5 to 40 cents → drone starts as a tight pad and slowly blossoms into a chord cloud.

vector_grain.description: Load a vocal or orchestral source, grain size medium, grain position drifting slowly (via Vector), feedback high. Each playback degrades slightly — the source DECAYS as the piece plays.
Grain density maximum, pitch spread wide, position random. The source is obliterated into texture — useful when the sample is too recognizable.
Grain size long (500-1000ms), density low (1-2 grains), pitch ratio 0.5 or 0.25. Effectively time-stretches the source with lo-fi grain character. Stars of the Lid territory.

tension.description: Plectrum exciter, string damping high, body=acoustic guitar, noise low. Useful as a percussion-melody hybrid when acoustic guitar sampling feels sterile.
Bow exciter, slow speed, pressure medium, body=cello, full sustain. Pair with Convolution Reverb hall IR for Jóhann Jóhannsson / Max Richter territory.
Bow on a high-tension thin string, no damping, infinite sustain — the sound is pure harmonic series. Useful under sustained pads as hidden-voice color.
Real producers don't draw straight lines or perfect curves. Their automation reflects human movement.
Every great minimal track has 2-3 moments where something unexpected happens — a sound that doesn't belong, a brief disruption that breaks the hypnosis just enough to deepen it.

arrangement_looper.description: Playback approaches breakdown → toggle 1-bar loop → hold for as long as needed → release → continue playback.

rotating_rhythm_generator.description: Kick: ring 16 / pulses 4 / rotation 0. Snare: ring 16 / pulses 2 / rotation 8. Hat: ring 13 / pulses 7 / rotation 3. Asymmetric groove that sits together but never locks.

prearranger.description: Place empty slots every 16 bars across a 60-minute arrangement → press record → improvise → each section lands in its slot.
Build the arrangement scaffold based on your setlist → perform → recording is auto-organized.

performer.description: Map one fader to filter cutoff, send level, reverb decay, sat drive simultaneously → turn = whole chord chain morphs.
Build a crossfader that switches between two device states via inverse macro curves.
Map a footpedal input via MIDI → Performer fader → filter open, send boost, reverb decay extend, LFO depth rise. One pedal drives an arrangement energy shift.

cv_envelope_follower.description: Route kick drum to CV Envelope Follower → CV output to pad's filter cutoff → pad brightens with every kick, darkens between.
Route vocal to CV Envelope Follower → invert output → modulate reverb wet → reverb ducks when vocal is present, blooms in silences.

variations.description: Save 5 effect-chain states on a chord stab (different reverbs, pitches, filters). Clip-trigger morph between them every 2 bars. Result: chord stab is subtly different every time it plays.
Bind scenes to Variation snapshots — firing a scene also morphs the whole device chain.

cv_shaper.description: Need a parameter envelope shape that's not on the device? Draw it in CV Shaper, route to the parameter. Unlimited curve flexibility.

cv_instrument.technique: Modular voice in a DAW session
cv_instrument.description: Assign MIDI track → CV Instrument → modular oscillator → modular filter → return audio back to Live track. Modular voice on the timeline.

cv_lfo.description: Drop 3x CV LFO on a return track, each at different rates (0.07Hz, 0.23Hz, 1.11Hz), route outputs to filter/amp/pitch → organic drift that doesn't repeat.

cv_utility.description: Put CV Utility between an LFO and a filter → scale down to 0.3 → same LFO, subtler effect.

surround_panner.description: Hi-hat bus → Surround Panner in quad → auto-Rotate at slow rate → hats circle the dance floor.

vector_map.description: Particle X → filter cutoff, particle Y → reverb wet, particle velocity → dry/wet balance. Single physical motion drives a 3-parameter sonic change.
Set strong gravity toward center + low friction → particle orbits return to origin with decay. Map to pad tone controls → pad breathes in-and-out toward a rest state.
High initial velocity + medium friction → particle wanders, eventually settles. Map to filter + detune → synth starts mobile, finds a rest-state timbre over 16 bars.

pitch.description: The difference between "sounds like a computer" and "sounds alive" is modulation that operates below conscious perception.
In dub techno, reverb and delay are not decorations — they generate the harmonic and melodic content.
Minimal-techno producers build textures by layering tiny, filtered, processed fragments — each one barely audible alone, together they create a living fabric.
Every great minimal track has 2-3 moments where something unexpected happens — a sound that doesn't belong, a brief disruption that breaks the hypnosis just enough to deepen it.
In experimental electronic music, effects chains ARE instruments.

echo.description: In dub techno, reverb and delay are not decorations — they generate the harmonic and melodic content.

reverb.description: In dub techno, reverb and delay are not decorations — they generate the harmonic and melodic content.
Sidechain compression is not just for the kick-bass pump. It's a modulation source.
Minimal-techno producers build textures by layering tiny, filtered, processed fragments — each one barely audible alone, together they create a living fabric.
Don't think about tracks — think about frequency bands. Each band is a voice in the mix.
Real producers don't draw straight lines or perfect curves. Their automation reflects human movement.
Every great minimal track has 2-3 moments where something unexpected happens — a sound that doesn't belong, a brief disruption that breaks the hypnosis just enough to deepen it.
In experimental electronic music, effects chains ARE instruments.

delay.description: In dub techno, reverb and delay are not decorations — they generate the harmonic and melodic content.
Every great minimal track has 2-3 moments where something unexpected happens — a sound that doesn't belong, a brief disruption that breaks the hypnosis just enough to deepen it.
In experimental electronic music, effects chains ARE instruments.

chord.description: In dub techno, reverb and delay are not decorations — they generate the harmonic and melodic content.
Don't think about tracks — think about frequency bands. Each band is a voice in the mix.

multiband_dynamics.description: Sidechain compression is not just for the kick-bass pump. It's a modulation source.

envelope_follower.description: Sidechain compression is not just for the kick-bass pump. It's a modulation source.

compressor.description: Sidechain compression is not just for the kick-bass pump. It's a modulation source.
In experimental electronic music, effects chains ARE instruments.

random.description: Real producers don't draw straight lines or perfect curves. Their automation reflects human movement.

convolution_reverb.description: In experimental electronic music, effects chains ARE instruments.

gate.description: In experimental electronic music, effects chains ARE instruments.
